title: Make artifact transfer redaction total and self-contained
goal: Make artifact push and pull error handling total, bounded and redacted even when the operator module does not expose a private redaction helper, while preserving create-only destination and exact SHA-256 verification contracts.

approval.note: Repair response shaping and redaction only. Do not weaken host registration, create-only writes or source/destination digest verification.

acceptance.assertion: Artifact transfer uses a public shared redaction function or an injected callable with a defined fallback; no path dereferences an optional private operator attribute.
Local validation, SSH/SCP failure, destination collision, source drift and destination hash mismatch each return a bounded redacted error without NameError, AttributeError or leaking command output.
Successful push and pull still require registered hosts, create-only destination semantics, expected source SHA-256 and independently verified destination SHA-256.
Tests cover an operator with and without redaction helpers, secret-bearing stderr, missing SSH host, transfer failure, collision, hash mismatch and successful exact transfer.
A deployed transfer to a registered SSH host succeeds with matching source and destination hashes, while an induced failure returns only bounded redacted diagnostics.

live_reproduction.context: A create-only SHA-bound push from heim-pc to heimserver failed inside response redaction before a trustworthy transfer receipt could be produced.

does_not_establish: that SSH connectivity was otherwise healthy
permission to treat an unverified copy as delivered
